subjects.name: General Aptitude (Qualifying)
subjects.analysis: Qualifying nature only. Marks not added to merit. Reasoning, Quant, and English must clear sectional cutoffs.

subjects.name: Professional Knowledge - IT
subjects.analysis: Core merit section. Focus on systems architecture, security, and data management.

subjects.name: Professional Knowledge - Law
subjects.analysis: Legal merit section. Deep focus on Banking Acts and Recovery proceedings.

subjects.name: Professional Knowledge - Finance
subjects.analysis: Finance merit section. Focus on Auditing, Accounting Standards, and Credit.

mentorship_data.title: Written Exam Strategy
mentorship_data.content: Aptitude is only a gateway. Since it is qualifying, reach safe marks and spend 90% time on Professional Knowledge.

mentorship_data.title: Document & PQE Logic
mentorship_data.content: SBI calculates experience from the date of final result. Ensure your certificates match the exact niche of the post code.

mentorship_data.title: Interview Prep
mentorship_data.content: The panel consists of domain experts. Be ready for technical cross-questioning on your past projects and industry trends.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Are Aptitude marks added to merit?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No, Phase I Aptitude (Quant/Reasoning/English) is only for qualifying; merit is based on PK and Interview.
Yes, for most Specialist Officer roles, 2 to 8 years of post-qualification experience is required.
Salary is revised; JMGS-I Gross is ~85k and MMGS-III can exceed 1.4 Lakhs.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Can I apply for multiple posts?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No, a candidate can only apply for one post code per recruitment project.
For permanent roles, the probation is typically 6 months to 2 years.
